content:
Startup gate first reply MUST confirm: (1) you are in `browser-use` agent mode, (2) your model is minimax-MiniMax-M3.

You are the BUILDER for an autodoc.es above-the-fold vehicle-search-selector clone.

User Goal (verbatim):
> Clone the main above-the-fold header section of https://www.autodoc.es/, specifically isolating the complete interactive vehicle search selector component (the multi-tab interface for selecting Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles, etc., by make, model, and engine). Save the full component inside './autodoc-clone'. It must be functionally and visually identical down to the pixel level, replicating all layout structures, dynamic dropdown behaviors, fonts, exact color tokens, hover states, animations, and any embedded media or layout effects.

Scope: IN = site header/nav + the interactive vehicle-search selector (tabs, dropdowns, CTA, decorative background). OUT = everything below the fold.

Workspace: work dir `/app/clone-web`. References: `/app/clone-web/clone_guide_v2.md` and `/app/clone-web/clone_landing_page_101.md` — read BOTH first. Output project: `/app/autodoc-clone/` (Vite + React + Tailwind). Plans: `/app/clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md`.

Process (per guide_v2 phases):
1. Phase 1 — Visual discovery. Navigate to https://www.autodoc.es/, resize 1440×900 (and 1920×1080), wait 8–10s, screenshot the above-the-fold only. Run Sniper CSS scripts (clone_landing_page_101.md §5) on every selector element. Detect canvas/video/iframes under the hero. Capture fonts via @font-face and performance.getEntriesByType('resource').
2. Phase 2 — Plan. Write `/app/clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md` with: tab list + order, dropdown fields per tab, exact color/typography/spacing tokens, assets to extract (logo, fonts, background media), animation/hover strategy, file structure, open questions. Do NOT start coding yet — wait for the supervisor to approve after merging plans with the evaluator.
3. Phase 3 — Build (after supervisor approval): scaffold `autodoc-clone/` with Vite + React + Tailwind. Self-host extracted fonts in `autodoc-clone/public/fonts/`. Build components matching exact tokens. Use Tailwind arbitrary values (`text-[14px]`, `tracking-[-0.2px]`, etc.). Wire interactive dropdowns (click to open, ESC/outside-click to close, make→model→engine cascading — populate from a small JSON derived from the original site's autocomplete or the most-common values).
4. Phase 4 — Hand off. Stop at "compiles and renders without console errors." Do NOT self-evaluate. Report files changed + build output + dev-server URL + the compact token sheet.

CRITICAL rules:
- Surgical edits only. No full-landing-page clone — confirm the rendered output stops at the selector block.
- Trust the live DOM over your memory.
- Do NOT write eval reports.
- Keep Spanish labels (the site is Spanish; translating breaks pixel parity).

Begin Phase 1 now. Reply with the startup gate check + your first screenshot plan.

**Startup gate:**
1. ⚠️ I am **not** in `browser-use` agent mode — I don't have a browser-automation tool (no Playwright/Puppeteer/headless Chrome) in this environment. I have `read_image`, `web_search`, and `fetch_url`, which is enough to read the rendered HTML and analyze a static screenshot from disk, but not enough to drive a live browser at specific viewports, run JS, intercept network responses, or read `performance.getEntriesByType('resource')` and live `@font-face` declarations the way a real browser does.
2. ✅ Model is **minimax-MiniMax-M3**.

This is a constraint you need to know before I start writing code that pretends to be pixel-accurate. Below is the plan and the honest shortcut I'll take.

---

## Phase 1 — Visual discovery: what I can actually do

### Tools I have for this phase
- `fetch_url` on `https://www.autodoc.es/` → returns the SSR/initial HTML as markdown. Static only — anything injected by client JS (the full vehicle-search selector is almost certainly hydrated by JS; the SSR markup is usually just a shell) will be missing or partial.
- `web_search` → can find the site's autocomplete JSON endpoints, font hosts, CDN, etc.
- `read_image` → only useful if *you* take a screenshot and drop it into the workspace.
- `read_file` / `bash` / `grep` → for the reference guides in `/app/clone-web/`.

### Tools I do NOT have
- ❌ Browser control (no resize-to-1440×900, no `waitForNetworkIdle`, no JS execution, no live DOM/CSSOM inspection, no `getEntriesByType('resource')`).
- ❌ Per-element computed-style extraction at runtime.
- ❌ Video/canvas/iframe detection in a live page.
- ❌ Sniper CSS script execution.

### My proposed Phase 1 (honest)

| Step | Action | Output |
|------|--------|--------|
| 1a | `read_file` both reference guides end-to-end. | Internalized constraints. |
| 1b | `fetch_url` `https://www.autodoc.es/` with `enable_llm=True` to get structure: header markup, visible tab labels, dropdown field names, CTA text, footer of header section, any inline color/font hints. | Structural map. |
| 1c | `web_search` for `[Autodoc ES vehicle selector tabs make model engine]` and inspect the autocomplete endpoint shape (e.g., `/api/vehicle/...`). | JSON shape for cascading data. |
| 1d | `web_search` for `[Autodoc fonts typekit google fonts site:autodoc.es]` and `gtag` / `segment` patterns to find font provider + CDN host so I can pull WOFF2 files directly. | Font download URLs. |
| 1e | If you can drop a 1440×900 screenshot at `/app/clone-web/ref-hero.png`, I'll `read_image` it and extract exact colors, spacing, and typography from the pixels. **This is the only way I get pixel-truthful tokens.** | Token sheet. |
| 1f | Write Phase-2 plan with explicit gaps marked `ASSUMED — needs screenshot confirmation`. | `/app/clone-web/team-log/builder_plan.md`. |

### What this means for pixel-parity

The user-stated bar is "functionally and visually identical down to the pixel level." With no browser, I cannot measure:
- exact `getComputedStyle().color` values,
- exact `font-weight` / `letter-spacing`,
- exact background-image / gradient stops inside the header,
- any `canvas` / `video` elements used as decoration,
- the actual timing/keyframes of hover and tab-switch animations.

I'll infer as much as I can from the HTML + your screenshot, but anything I can't measure I will mark as `ASSUMED` in the plan and the final token sheet — not silently guess.

---

## Confirm before I proceed

Three quick decisions so I don't waste tokens:

1. **Screenshot**: can you drop `/app/clone-web/ref-hero.png` (1440×900 above-the-fold, autodoc.es with cookies/consent dismissed if needed)? If yes, pixel tokens come from that file.
2. **Acceptable to start Phase 1 with `fetch_url` only** (no live browser), knowing the SSR markup may be thin and the token sheet will carry `ASSUMED` flags? Or do you want to escalate to a real browser tool first?
3. **Autocomplete data**: OK to ship the cascading dropdowns with a small hand-curated JSON of the top ~20 makes per tab (Passenger Cars / Trucks / Motorcycles / …) instead of reverse-engineering the live API? This is what plan §3 already implies; I just want explicit confirmation before I bake it in.

Ready to execute steps 1a–1d as soon as you confirm (or correct) the above.
